$schema: Strategy Matrix - Maps Strategy x InvestorType to allowed/excluded combos with underwriting parameters

family-office-extended.reason: Core matches long-hold, low-management-burden, and wealth-preservation mandate; after-tax return must be computed alongside pre-tax IRR

individual-hnw.reason: Core appropriate for HNW individual seeking stable cash flow with limited operational involvement; illiquidity period typically manageable

small-operator.reason: Core assets (stabilized, low-maintenance) align with small operator's limited management bandwidth; recourse debt still standard at this investor scale

family-office-extended.reason: Core-plus light value-add acceptable if operating partner is in place and execution risk is contained; after-tax return computed alongside pre-tax

individual-hnw.reason: Core-plus acceptable for HNW individual if value-add execution does not require hands-on involvement; education component on execution risk required

small-operator.reason: Core-plus is primary sweet spot for small operators -- light renovation or lease-up within self-management capability; personal guaranty modeled at this LTV level

family-office-extended.reason: Value-add execution intensity exceeds operational simplicity mandate; requires dedicated operating partner with full accountability -- only permitted as co-invest with institutional-quality operator and explicit family office IC approval

conditions: Execution managed entirely by experienced sponsor -- no hands-on involvement from investor
Business plan must be explainable in plain language
Illiquidity period clearly defined and within 7-year max
Single investment does not exceed 15% of liquid net worth
individual-hnw.reason: Value-add as passive LP investment acceptable; direct ownership of value-add property by individual HNW investor excluded unless investor has operating capability

conditions: Value-add scope must be within operator's self-management capability (unit refreshes, minor renovation -- not gut rehab)
Capital reserve must cover full renovation budget plus 20% contingency before acquisition closes
DSCR at stabilized NOI must pass 1.20 threshold; in-place DSCR at 1.00 minimum
Deferred maintenance must be fully scoped -- not estimated -- prior to LOI
small-operator.reason: Light value-add is core competency for small operators managing their own properties; heavy renovation or repositioning exceeds typical small operator capability and capital

family-office-extended.reason: Opportunistic strategies require full-time operational intensity, development risk, or entitlement execution that directly conflicts with operational simplicity mandate and long-hold bias; excluded without exception

individual-hnw.reason: Opportunistic deal complexity, leverage levels, illiquidity duration, and execution risk are mismatched with individual HNW investor risk profile, liquidity constraints, and education requirements; excluded

small-operator.reason: Opportunistic strategies (ground-up development, entitlement plays, distressed workout) require institutional capital reserves, specialized expertise, and organizational capacity that small operators (1-10 properties) do not have; excluded
